assertions.expectation: Explains that exact upgrade dates are difficult to predict but several tools help narrow the window
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explicitly acknowledge that exact upgrade dates are difficult to predict; instead presents solutions as if they provide exact predictability

assertions.expectation: Mentions the GKE release schedule as a 'best case' timeline — upgrades won't happen before listed dates
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the GKE release schedule or explain it as a 'best case' timeline for when upgrades won't happen before certain dates

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the cluster's auto-upgrade status for the current target version and any pauses
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention checking auto-upgrade status for current target version or any pauses, though it does show how to disable auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Mentions that new releases roll out progressively across regions over 4-5 business days, and the version promotion path includes channel promotion (Rapid → Regular → Stable)
assertions.reason: Response explains the channel progression (RAPID to REGULAR to STABLE) with timing estimates, though doesn't explicitly mention the 4-5 business day regional rollout

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows so upgrades only happen during known time slots. May note that for ultimate predictability, customers can initiate upgrades themselves instead of waiting for auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Response clearly shows how to configure maintenance windows and demonstrates manual upgrade control for ultimate predictability

assertions.expectation: Mentions scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance, opt-in via Cloud Logging) or the get-upgrade-info API for checking target versions programmatically
assertions.reason: Response mentions general upgrade notifications and monitoring but doesn't specifically mention the 72-hour advance notifications or the get-upgrade-info API

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE upgrade visibility shows the cluster's target version and EoL timeline
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention GKE upgrade visibility features or how to view target version and EoL timeline information

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing (advanced feature, limited adoption) as an option for multi-cluster predictability — not a primary recommendation
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention rollout sequencing as an advanced feature for multi-cluster predictability
